questions.questionText: The most common thyroid malignancy is:
questions.explanation: Papillary carcinoma is the most common thyroid cancer and typically spreads via lymphatics. Follicular carcinoma more often spreads hematogenously. Medullary carcinoma arises from parafollicular C cells. Anaplastic carcinoma is highly aggressive but much less common.

questions.questionText: Injury to which nerve during thyroid surgery leads to inability to produce high-pitched sounds?
questions.optionA: External branch of superior laryngeal nerve
questions.optionB: Internal branch of superior laryngeal nerve
questions.explanation: The external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve supplies cricothyroid, which tenses the vocal cords and is important for high-pitched phonation. Internal branch is sensory above the vocal cords. Hypoglossal and glossopharyngeal nerves do not control pitch in this context.

questions.questionText: A solitary thyroid nodule with medullary carcinoma should prompt evaluation for which associated endocrine disorder?
questions.explanation: Medullary carcinoma may occur as part of MEN 2 syndromes, which are strongly associated with pheochromocytoma. Identifying pheochromocytoma before surgery is essential to avoid perioperative catastrophe. Primary hyperaldosteronism, diabetes insipidus, and acromegaly are not the classic association.
questions.source: Schwartz’s Principles of Surgery, Thyroid and Parathyroid
